name: Co-Ne
description_short: CO-NE Federal Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit credit union serving Sedgwick and Deuel County residents in Colorado with basic deposit and lending services.
description_long:
CO-NE Federal Credit Union is a community-focused federal credit union located in Julesburg, Colorado, operating under the "People Helping People" cooperative philosophy. The institution serves residents of Sedgwick and Deuel County, with membership eligibility extended to family and relatives of existing members regardless of geographic location. The credit union maintains a small-town presence with a single physical branch and operates during standard business hours Monday through Friday.

CO-NE FCU offers core credit union services including member deposit accounts (passbook accounts and share certificates), home banking through their Member.Net platform, and standard lending products typical of community credit unions. The institution emphasizes personal service and local accessibility for members in rural Colorado communities. New members can join with a minimum $50 deposit into a passbook account, along with standard identification requirements (Social Security card and photo ID).

The credit union distinguishes itself through its explicit community focus and cooperative membership model, serving a specific geographic region rather than operating as a large multi-state institution. Their emphasis on the "People Helping People" philosophy reflects traditional credit union values of member ownership and reinvestment in the community. The organization is actively modernizing its digital offerings, as evidenced by their Member.Net home banking platform and website redesign initiatives.

CO-NE FCU is best suited for residents and family members of Sedgwick and Deuel County who value local community banking and personal relationships over digital-first services. The institution's rural location and modest size mean more limited product offerings and technology capabilities compared to larger regional or national credit unions. Members should verify current rates, loan products, and service offerings directly, as the website explicitly notes ongoing redesign and incomplete information.
services: Standard credit union lending (specific products not detailed on website)
Federal holiday closures and standard business operations
Member newsletters and rate information (in process of making accessible)
Account management and member service by phone
pros: NCUA-insured member deposits provide federal protection up to $250,000
Low minimum deposit requirement of $50 to establish membership
Member-owned cooperative structure means profits are returned to members rather than shareholders
Modern online banking through Member.Net platform for convenient account access
Extended membership eligibility to family members regardless of geographic location
Local Colorado presence with direct community relationships and personalized service
No-fee membership model typical of credit union structure
cons: Single physical branch location limits accessibility for non-local members
Limited business hours (9 AM-4 PM weekdays only) restrict access for working members
Website explicitly notes incomplete information and ongoing redesign, limiting product transparency
Small institution likely offers fewer loan products and rates compared to larger credit unions
Geographic membership restrictions limit eligibility for most applicants outside Sedgwick/Deuel County and family connections
best_for: Sedgwick and Deuel County residents seeking local community banking relationships
Existing members' family members and relatives living anywhere needing credit union membership
Rural Colorado consumers preferring personal service over large-scale banking institutions
Members seeking basic deposit products and standard credit union lending services
diagnosis: CO-NE FCU is best for Sedgwick and Deuel County residents or their family members seeking straightforward community credit union services with personal relationships and NCUA protection. Primary caveat: the website is incomplete and undergoing redesign, so potential members must contact the credit union directly at (970) 474-2617 to verify current rates, specific loan products, and service offerings before joining.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
